notes: Canonical Z3×Z2 Cayley table. Entry[i][j] = componentwise addition in Z3×Z2. shapeIndex=floor(v/2), fillIndex=v%2. Specified in task brief.
Cayley table of Z6. Entry[i][j] = (i+j) mod 6. Z6 ≅ Z3×Z2 so isotopic to z3xz2-algebraic, but different structure as a Latin square.
Cayley table of S3 / D3. Elements: 0=e, 1=r, 2=r², 3=s, 4=sr, 5=sr², where r³=e, s²=e, srs=r⁻¹. Non-abelian: different isotopy class from Z6/Z3×Z2.
S3 Cayley table with symbol permutation [0,3,1,4,2,5] applied. Same isotopy class as s3-dihedral but distinct seed for scrambling diversity.
Z3×Z2 with row permutation [2,0,4,1,5,3] applied. Isotopic to z3xz2-algebraic; provides a distinct starting layout for gameplay variety.
S3 with row permutation [3,1,4,0,5,2] applied. Isotopic to s3-dihedral; distinct layout for scrambling.
Z3×Z2 with column permutation [3,1,5,0,4,2] applied. Isotopic to z3xz2-algebraic; distinct layout.
Back-circulant (negacirculant). Entry[i][j] = (j-i+6) mod 6. Isotopic to Z6; distinct layout for scrambling diversity.
